Notes on Side effects of smoking.Definition: Smoking is the inhalation of tobacco smoke, which contains thousands of chemicals, many of which are harmful to the body.
Notes on Side effects of smoking.Key_concepts:
1. Respiratory damage – affects lungs and airways.
2. Cardiovascular harm – increases heart disease risk.
3. Cancer risk – especially lung, throat, and mouth.
4. Reduced immunity – makes infections easier.
5. Skin and appearance – causes wrinkles and yellowing.
6. Pregnancy effects – harms fetal development.
Notes on Side effects of smoking.Important_points:
- Smoking damages the cilia in lungs, leading to chronic cough and infections.
- Nicotine causes blood vessels to narrow, raising blood pressure.
- Carcinogens in smoke cause DNA mutations that lead to cancer.
- Smokers have weaker immune cells, so colds and flu last longer.
- Smoking stains teeth and skin, and accelerates skin aging.
- Pregnant smokers increase risk of miscarriage, low birth weight, and birth defects.
Notes on Side effects of smoking.Short_summary: Smoking harms nearly every organ: it clogs lungs, raises heart risk, fuels cancer, weakens immunity, and ages skin. Even small amounts increase these dangers, and it can harm unborn babies. Quitting early saves life and improves health.
